comment_pin: allowManagedPermissionRulesOnly + allowManagedHooksOnly MUST sit at this TOP level: claude-code reads them ONLY at the settings root (verified against the shipped binary v2.1.205 at every read site); nested inside permissions{} they are silently INERT — which is how a stray user-layer ask rule stalled an unattended run on 2026-07-09. With the pin LIVE, user/project/local/cliArg/session permission rules (allow+ask+deny) AND user-layer hooks are ALL ignored — so everything the loop needs lives in THIS file: the stall-visibility loggers, the one destructive ask, and the tool allows. Session-added ask rules (the stall class) are structurally impossible.

permissions.comment_allow: UNSHACKLED (P0, 2026-07-11): allow ALL Bash + the file/orchestration tools and run defaultMode=default so the interactive agent executes its own commands WITHOUT prompts. The interactive gate-push PreToolUse hook AND the auto-classifier are BOTH removed — they were the per-iteration human clicks the objective forbids (the classifier asked on compound/piped/interpreter commands; the hook TEXT-SCANNED for push/merge verbs and false-positived, e.g. the word 'merged' inside an echo). Merge-to-main safety is preserved by THREE PRECISE controls, none of which stops normal work: (1) the `require-PR` server ruleset on main — nothing direct-pushes main; (2) the `Bash(gh pr merge:*)` DENY below — the interactive agent cannot hand-merge a PR to main; it is an AUTO-DENY (never a prompt) and a precise prefix rule (never false-positives on 'merged' in text), unlike the removed text-scanning hook; (3) the headless poller (plain shell — it bypasses THESE managed settings) does every AUTONOMOUS merge under its two INDEPENDENT gates (host live-gate App + fitness App, distinct identities). HONEST RESIDUAL (do not overclaim): the deny stops the DIRECT merge verb only — a raw-API merge (`gh api PUT .../pulls/N/merge`, GraphQL, curl) remains POSSIBLE under Bash(*), and the require-PR ruleset does not block API merges. This residual is KNOWN and ACCEPTED per the zero-gate decision: pattern-denying raw API shapes would be prefix-fragile sieve-theater (ANTI-THEATER doctrine — a guard implying coverage it can't deliver is worse than none), and recovery from a bad merge is AUTOMATIC (require-PR keeps main fully revertible + git revert + the host health-gate digest rollback + fitness's standing preserve-recoverability rule). The deny[] also blocks the package-manager escape hatches + $PATH-shadow writes. The sole surviving ask is `gh repo delete`.
